response.justification: The candidate brings genuine geospatial domain experience (founder of GISCollective, Green Map GIS work, FOSS4G speaker) and proven low-latency systems engineering at Sociomantic handling millions of requests per second with async I/O, fibers, and caching. However, that systems work was in DLang, not C++; his C++ experience is limited to a short 2014-2015 stint at National Instruments and a bachelor thesis, and his recent years are dominated by TypeScript/Node.js product work. He has no direct routing, tiling, or navigation guidance experience, so the fit with the core C++/Rust routing pipeline requirement is uncertain.
strengths: Real geospatial data platform experience as founder of GISCollective, an open-source GIS platform used by Green Map
Low-latency, high-throughput distributed systems work (millions of HTTP requests/second) with async I/O and caching at Sociomantic
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling authorship, and demonstrated willingness to learn across the stack
concerns: Modern C++ experience is dated (2014-2015) and brief; no Rust; primary systems language is DLang
No routing, map tiling, or navigation guidance system experience despite adjacent GIS mapping work
Recent roles (2020-present) are TypeScript/Node.js web and pricing product work, far from routing data pipelines

key_factors.factor: Geospatial platform and GIS domain experience
